name: analyze-file-structure
description: Analyze the structure of a single TypeScript/TSX file to see its exported functions, components, interfaces, and key code sections. Useful when working with large files or before making changes to understand what's inside.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the file to analyze (e.g., /home/jewelshadow/Documents/Projects/Folio/folio-reader/components/SeriesCard.tsx)

when_to_use: Before editing a large file to understand its structure
When looking for specific functions or components in a file
To check what a file exports before importing from it
To find line numbers of specific code sections
When refactoring to see what needs to be moved or updated
output: List of exported items with line numbers
follow_up_actions: Read specific line ranges to see implementation details
Use line numbers to target edits precisely
Check for hardcoded colors or duplicate patterns
